response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, hands-on senior engineer with roughly 19 years of experience, satisfying the minimum bar of 15 years and a technical bachelor's degree. However, the role is a director-level IC position centered on AI strategy, and his profile does not demonstrate that altitude: his titles top out at Staff/Senior developer, his AI exposure is recent (about a year of AI-agent tooling and MCP work) rather than recognized expertise with shipped AI products or platforms, and there is no evidence of advising strategic customers, shaping cloud product strategy, or operating at principal scope. An unexplained employment gap between January 2020 and September 2020 also warrants clarification. Strong fit for senior/staff product engineering roles, but not this one.
strengths: 19+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Genuine entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used in production
Active knowledge-sharer with conference talks, multi-day courses, and workshops, plus recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work
concerns: No demonstrated principal/director-level scope: career history is individual senior/staff development work, not organization-level technical strategy or customer advisory
AI experience is shallow and recent relative to the 'recognized AI expertise' requirement; no advanced degree in an AI-related field and no shipped AI products of note
Limited cloud-platform depth for a cloud/AI strategy role, and an unexplained ~8-month career gap in 2020

key_factors.factor: Seniority mismatch for director-level IC role
Limited recognized AI expertise and product track record
Meets minimum experience and education requirements with founder experience
